heading: Administration, protection, and development; statutory authorities for conservation and management of natural resources; Glen Canyon Dam and Reservoir
label: 460dd–3 Administration, protection, and development; statutory authorities for conservation and management of natural resources; Glen Canyon Dam and Reservoir
text: The Secretary shall administer, protect, and develop the recreation area in accordance with the provisions of the Act of (; et seq.), as amended and supplemented, and with any other statutory authority available to him for the conservation and management of natural resources to the extent he finds such authority will further the purposes of this subchapter: , That nothing in this subchapter shall affect or interfere with the authority of the Secretary granted by Public Law 485, Eighty-fourth Congress, second session [ et seq.], to operate Glen Canyon Dam and Reservoir in accordance with the purposes of the Colorado River Storage Project Act [ et seq.] for river regulation, irrigation, flood control, and generation of hydroelectric power.August 25, 191639 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1143 U.S.C. 62043 U.S.C. 620See References in Text note below.1Provided, however
notes: References in TextThe Act of (; et seq.), referred to in text, is , , known as the National Park Service Organic Act, which enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under , National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. L. 113–287128 Stat. 3094section 100101 of Title 54Public Law 485, Eighty-fourth Congress, second session, referred to in text, is , , which is popularly known as the Colorado River Storage Project Act, and which is classified generally to chapter 12B (§ 620 et seq.) of Title 43, Public Lands.
